figures.title: FinMM-LLM Framework Architecture
figures.description: Three-layer architecture diagram: Text Encoder (FinBERT) on left in blue, Time-Series Encoder (PatchTST) on right in orange, Cross-Modal Attention in center connecting both, Decision Head at bottom outputting portfolio signals.

figures.title: Cumulative Returns: Multi-Modal vs Single-Modal Strategies
figures.description: Cumulative log-return curves for three strategies plus FF5 benchmark over the out-of-sample period (2023-2024). Shaded regions for earnings announcement windows.

figures.title: Component Ablation: Impact of Text Encoder, TS Encoder, and Fusion Method
figures.description: Three grouped bar panels: (a) Text encoder variants (FinBERT vs GPT vs L-M), (b) TS encoder variants (PatchTST vs LSTM vs iTransformer), (c) Fusion methods (Cross-Attn vs Concat vs Ensemble). Y-axis: Sharpe ratio.

figures.title: Cross-Attention Heatmap Around Earnings Announcement
figures.description: Left panel: attention weights (text tokens × price patches) during earnings window [-5, +5] days. Right panel: same stock during normal period. Shows concentrated attention on sentiment-bearing tokens during earnings.

figures.title: Multi-Modal Attention Intensity vs Market Volatility (VIX)
figures.description: Dual-axis time-series: left axis shows rolling 20-day average cross-attention intensity, right axis shows VIX. Demonstrates positive correlation — multi-modal fusion becomes more important during volatile periods.
